summary: A brief biographical overview of David Hume's philosophy from a Goodreads book page. It outlines his core empiricist beliefs, including his skepticism of rational causality, his view that desire governs reason, and his conception of the self as a bundle of sensations.

principles.statement: Desire rather than reason governs human behavior.
principles.rationale: Human nature is driven by psychological desires and feelings, in direct opposition to rationalist views that prioritize reason.

principles.statement: Humans have knowledge only of things they directly experience.
principles.rationale: There are no innate ideas; all human knowledge must stem from direct empirical observation.

principles.statement: Inductive reasoning and causality cannot be justified rationally.
principles.rationale: Our belief in cause and effect comes from custom and constant conjunction, rather than inherent logic.

principles.statement: Ethics are based on feelings rather than abstract moral principles.
principles.rationale: Morality is a matter of sentiment and human feeling rather than rational deduction.

mental_models.name: Bundle Theory of the Self
mental_models.description: The concept that humans have no actual conception of a unified, persistent self, but rather only experience a bundle of sensations associated with the self.

mental_models.name: Constant Conjunction
mental_models.description: The lens through which we perceive causality: we assume cause and effect not because of inherent logic, but because we are accustomed to seeing two events frequently happening together.

anti_patterns.description: Believing that reason governs human behavior.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Human behavior is actually governed by desire and psychological factors, not rational deduction.

anti_patterns.description: Assuming the existence of innate ideas.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Humans only possess knowledge of things they have directly experienced.

anti_patterns.description: Attempting to justify causality and inductive reasoning rationally.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Causality is merely an assumption resulting from custom and constant conjunction, not logic.
